ORDER DENYING DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STAY OR DISMISS ACTION FOR INCONVENIENT FORUM

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT ACTIVE PRODUCTION AND DESIGN, INC.'S MOTION TO STAY OR DISMISS FOR INCONVENIENT FORUM IS DENIED. WHILE THE PARTIES AGREE THAT GEORGIA IS A SUITABLE ALTERNATIVE FORUM, ACTIVE HAS NOT MET ITS BURDEN OF PROOF THAT THIS CASE SHOULD BE HEARD IN GEORGIA RATHER THAN CALIFORNIA. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (SEE MINI-MINUTES AND/OR ORDER FOR COMPLETE RULING). JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976 =302/HK

MINI MINUTES FOR NOV-03-2017 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION, 302, PLAINTIFF UTP PRODUCTIONS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND/OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE ISSUE OF LIABILITY AGAINST DEFENDANT ACTIVE PRODUCTION AND DESIGN, INC. ARE DENIED IN THEIR ENTIRETY. UTP HAS NOT PROVIDED SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO MEET ITS SUMMARY JUDGMENT BURDEN TO SHOW THAT THE PAYROLL SERVICE AGREEMENT IS AN ENFORCEABLE CONTRACT BETWEEN UTP AND ACTIVE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (SEE MINI-MINUTES AND/OR ORDER FOR COMPLETE RULING). JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: G. GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: REYES F. HUNTER, CSR #6576, EMAIL:RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Nov-03-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Desa Ballard, Esq. of Ballard & Watson, Tel. 803.796.9299, for plaintiff and moving party;
John S. Rueppel, Esq. of O'Neill Rueppel LLP, Tel 415.992.3123, for defendant and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF UTP PRODUCTIONS, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND/OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION, as follows: Plaintiff UTP Productions, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and its alternative motion for summary adjudication on the issue of liability against defendant Active Production and Design, Inc. are denied in their entirety. UTP has not provided sufficient evidence to meet its summary judgment burden to show that the Payroll Service Agreement is an enforceable contract between UTP and Active. UTP also failed to provide sufficient evidence to show that it can recover from Active based on Active's Collective Bargaining Agreement with Local 16. There are also triable disputes regarding: 1) the terms of the agreement between UTP and Active; 2) whether Active breached those terms by failing to object to the timesheets prior to UTP's processing of the payroll and by refusing to reimburse UTP for the full amount of the claimed outstanding balance; and 3) the amount of UTP's damages. UTP's requested summary adjudication of liability only also runs afoul of CCP 473c's limitation on what may be summarily adjudicated.

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Court Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR #6576, email:rhunter@sftc.org.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-02-2017 Set For Trial On Dec-04-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Or Dismiss Action For Inconvenient Forum
Off Calendar Jan-05-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. THOMAS H. SCHELLY OF THE LIPELES LAW GROUP, APC, TEL. NO. 1310-322-2211 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. JOHN S. RUEPPEL OF THE O'NEILL & RUEPPEL LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-992-3123 APPEARING FOR THE
DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Active Production and Design, Inc.'s motion to stay or dismiss for inconvenient forum is denied. While the parties agree that Georgia is a suitable alternative forum, Active has not met its burden of proof that this case should be heard in Georgia rather than California. The only evidence Active submits relevant to the private and public interest factors is a declaration by its president and CEO stating plaintiff UTP Productions, Inc. and Active are not parties to a contract, all of Active's employees reside in Georgia, and Active is a Georgia corporation. As to the private interest factors, UTP has shown it would be expensive to litigate in Georgia since there are many California residents who assertedly provided labor for and are witnesses to the work performed in San Francisco which is the subject of this lawsuit. As to the public interest factors, UTP has submitted evidence showing California has an interest in adjudicating this claim because the disputed contract was performed in San Francisco and involved many California residents. Moreover, although not dispositive, UTP's choice of a California forum is entitled to some weight. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/hk
